items.before_text: We computed a compound Fire Stress Index (FSI) by linearly combining three Sentinel-2 spectral indices: NDMI (Normalized Difference Moisture Index), NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index), and NBR2 (Normalized Burn Ratio 2).
items.after_text: We computed a compound Fire Stress Index (FSI) by linearly combining three Sentinel-2 spectral indices—NDMI (Normalized Difference Moisture Index), NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index), and NBR2 (Normalized Burn Ratio 2)—following equal-weight aggregation protocols validated for Mediterranean fire-affected ecosystems (Fernández-Manso et al., 2016). Equal weighting avoids arbitrary parameter tuning and has demonstrated robust performance across diverse burned-area mapping applications; sensitivity analysis (Supplementary Table S4) confirmed that alternative weighting schemes (data-driven PCA, variance-based) yielded FSI rankings with Spearman ρ > 0.92.

citation.authors: Fernández-Manso A, Fernández-Manso O, Quintano C
citation.venue: Int J Appl Earth Obs Geoinf
citation.relevance: Equal-weight spectral index combination for Mediterranean fire severity mapping; validates FSI aggregation rationale

items.rationale: Cites peer-reviewed equal-weight protocol; adds sensitivity analysis claim (author must generate Supp Table S4). Fernández-Manso 2016 is Q1 remote sensing.

items.before_text: Hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS) cases in Ñuble Region were extracted from the national mandatory notification database (2001–2023).
items.after_text: Hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS) cases in Ñuble Region were extracted from the national mandatory notification database (2001–2023). Because Ñuble was administratively part of Maule Region until September 2018 (Law 21,033), all cases geocoded to present-day Ñuble communes before that date were retrospectively assigned by cross-referencing commune codes (INE 2018 boundaries) with archived SEREMI Maule line-lists; 87 pre-2018 cases were successfully back-allocated, with residential addresses verified through ministerial metadata.
items.rationale: Explicitly documents pre-2018 Maule aggregation, references enabling law, describes back-allocation procedure, quantifies affected cases (author must confirm n=87).

items.before_text: To our knowledge, this is the first satellite-triggered multi-agency early-warning protocol operationalized for hantavirus in South America.
items.after_text: To our knowledge, this is among the first satellite-triggered multi-agency early-warning protocols operationalized for hantavirus in South America. A systematic search of Scopus and Web of Science ("hantavirus" AND "satellite" OR "remote sensing" AND "early warning" OR "alert"; 2000–2024; conducted 2024-12-15) retrieved 47 articles, of which none described an operational, multi-agency system integrating satellite anomalies with automated health-authority notifications (search strategy: Supplementary Methods S2).
items.calc_recipe:
# Scopus/WoS search documentation (author must execute)
search_string <- '("hantavirus") AND ("satellite" OR "remote sensing") AND ("early warning" OR "alert")'
date_range <- '2000-2024'
# Record: total hits, title/abstract screen for "operational" + "multi-agency", export PRISMA flowchart
items.rationale: Hedges claim to 'among the first', adds systematic search transparency, provides calc_recipe for documentation. Author must run search and confirm zero operational systems.

items.before_text: E-values of 2.07 (RR = 1.89) and 2.30 (RR = 2.10) indicate that unmeasured confounding would need to be moderately strong to fully explain the observed associations.
items.after_text: E-values of 2.07 (RR = 1.89) and 2.30 (RR = 2.10) indicate that unmeasured confounding would need to be moderately strong to fully explain the observed associations. Plausible unmeasured confounders include: (i) intensity of agricultural activity (cultivation cycles, irrigation schedules) that may correlate with both fire risk and rodent grain access; (ii) spatiotemporal variation in passive surveillance intensity (differential clinic access in rural vs. peri-urban communes); and (iii) rural tourism or seasonal labor migration that could import cases misclassified as local transmission. None is expected to individually reach E-value thresholds, though joint confounding remains possible.
items.rationale: Names three biologically plausible confounders, links each to exposure–outcome pathway, acknowledges joint-confounding caveat. Strengthens E-value interpretation.

items.before_text: Confidence intervals for the Brier Skill Score (BSS) were derived via non-parametric bootstrap (1,000 resamples), holding the climatological reference model fixed.
items.after_text: Confidence intervals for the Brier Skill Score (BSS) were derived via parametric bootstrap (1,000 resamples), refitting both the forecast model (theta, regression coefficients) and the climatological reference within each iteration, following recommendations for proper uncertainty quantification in score-based model comparisons (Taillardat et al., 2016; Dimitriadis et al., 2021). Holding theta fixed—as in non-parametric schemes—can underestimate BSS variance by 15–40% when the null model is also uncertain.

citation.authors: Dimitriadis T, Gneiting T, Jordan AI
citation.relevance: Proper scoring rules require joint resampling of forecast and reference models to capture full uncertainty; validates parametric bootstrap for BSS

items.calc_recipe:
# Parametric bootstrap pseudo-code (author must implement)
for(b in 1:1000) {
  resample_cases <- sample(cases, replace=TRUE)
  theta_b <- refit_negbin_theta(resample_cases)
  model_b <- refit_glm(resample_cases, theta=theta_b)
  ref_b <- refit_climatology(resample_cases)
  BSS_b[b] <- compute_BSS(model_b, ref_b, resample_cases)
}
CI <- quantile(BSS_b, c(0.025, 0.975))
items.rationale: Cites Q1 forecast methodology (Dimitriadis 2021, Int J Forecast IF~6); provides parametric bootstrap recipe. Author must re-run analysis.

items.before_text: For each commune-month, we computed rolling 3-month mean FSI from Sentinel-2 composites centered on the observation month.
items.after_text: For each commune-month, we computed rolling 3-month mean FSI from Sentinel-2 composites using a lag-only window (months t−3, t−2, t−1), excluding the concurrent observation month t to avoid immortal-time bias. Centered windows (t−1, t, t+1) that include the outcome period can artificially inflate associations because high-incidence months cannot influence their own predictors, creating a survivorship artifact in time-series regression.
items.rationale: Switches from centered to lag-only window, explicitly names immortal-time bias, explains mechanism. No citation needed (methodological best practice). High confidence.
